**Analysis:** Issue #4416 is an automatically created and managed tracking issue generated by GitHub Agentic Workflows (gh-aw) to collect threat detection warnings and failures across agentic workflow runs. The body explicitly states 'Do not close this issue manually' and 'No action to take - Do not assign to an agent.' It is not a user-reported SkiaSharp bug, feature request, or question — it is CI/automation infrastructure metadata.

**Recommendations:** **keep-open** — Auto-managed GitHub Agentic Workflows tracking issue. The body explicitly states 'Do not close this issue manually' and 'No action to take - Do not assign to an agent.' It is infrastructure metadata, not a triageable SkiaSharp issue.

## Analysis

### Technical Summary

Issue #4416 is an automatically created and managed tracking issue generated by GitHub Agentic Workflows (gh-aw) to collect threat detection warnings and failures across agentic workflow runs. The body explicitly states 'Do not close this issue manually' and 'No action to take - Do not assign to an agent.' It is not a user-reported SkiaSharp bug, feature request, or question — it is CI/automation infrastructure metadata.

### Rationale

Classified as type/question under area/Build because the issue is an automated CI/infrastructure tracking mechanism, not a real user-reported SkiaSharp defect or enhancement request. The closest available type label is type/question; the closest area is area/Build since it relates to CI workflows. Suggested action is keep-open because the issue body explicitly prohibits closure and is self-managed by automation.

### Key Signals

- "This issue is automatically managed by GitHub Agentic Workflows. Do not close this issue manually." — **issue body** (Auto-managed infrastructure issue — should remain open; no manual action required.)
- "No action to take - Do not assign to an agent." — **issue body** (The issue explicitly requests no triage action be taken.)
